data.name: PEM Electrolysis (Green Hydrogen)
data.description: Proton exchange membrane electrolysis using renewable electricity to split water into hydrogen and oxygen. Fast response time ideal for coupling with variable renewables.
data.status: Commercial; scaling rapidly; ITM, Plug, Siemens leading

data.name: Solid Oxide Electrolysis (SOEC)
data.description: High-temperature electrolysis (700-850°C) using ceramic membranes. Higher efficiency than PEM when waste heat is available (e.g., from nuclear or industrial processes).
data.status: Commercial (Bloom Energy, Topsoe); scaling for industrial applications

data.name: Hydrogen Fuel Cell (PEMFC)
data.description: Converts hydrogen to electricity with water as only emission. Used in vehicles (Toyota Mirai, Hyundai Nexo), buses, trucks, and stationary power.
data.status: Commercial; Toyota, Hyundai, Plug Power, Ballard leading

data.name: Underground Hydrogen Storage
data.description: Storing large volumes of hydrogen in salt caverns, depleted gas fields, or aquifers. Essential for seasonal energy storage at grid scale.
data.status: Demonstrated in salt caverns (Clemens Dome, Texas); HyUnder project evaluating European sites
